title: Reading and Use of English – Part 2
description: For questions 9–16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only ONE word in each gap.

content.text: The ancient world is full (0) mysteries, particularly in the realm of music. Gaps in our knowledge (9) to ancient musical traditions are significant, (10) the artifacts found by archaeologists offer some clues. || (11) the absence of written notation comparable to that of modern times, much of what we know comes from paintings, texts, and the occasional instrument. It is difficult (12) to ascertain the exact sounds these instruments produced, (13) technological reconstructions offer possibilities. Recent discoveries have shed light (14) musical practices, yet they raise (15) many questions as they answer. Scholars continue to debate (16) or not the scales used then resemble those of today.

example.text: The ancient world is full (0) mysteries,
example.explanation: The preposition 'of' is used to indicate what something contains or is made up of.

questions.explanation: The past participle 'related' is used to describe the relationship or connection to something.
The conjunction 'but' is used to introduce a contrast between two statements.
The preposition 'with' is used to indicate accompaniment or condition under which something happens.
The adverb 'then' is used for sequence, meaning at that time or after.
'Though' is a conjunction used to introduce a statement that contrasts with or seems to contradict something that has been said previously.
The preposition 'on' is used to show a relationship, in this context, to indicate a subject matter.
'As' is used in comparisons to signify equivalence in amount or degree.
The conjunction 'whether' is used to introduce a clause expressing an indirect question or choice.
